summary: RE-Audit confirms the shipped polish mostly improves mobile chat overlay chrome and interaction. Fix 1 (input row height) and Fix 3 (avatar/header) landed as expected; Fix 5 (header unification) landed with a cohesive top bar. Fix 4 (chip clipping) is partially validated through CSS but cannot be fully verified because chips aren’t rendering on first load due to the boot flow. Fix 2 (empty transcript chips on load) did not land in the live UI because chips never render on initial load, causing an empty-state deficiency. The dominant regression remains an end-to-end failure: sending a message yields a 500 server error from /api/agent-chat, with user-visible error text and a console/network signal of 500. The page is therefore not-shippable in its current state; the fastest path to ship-ready is to fix the API failure, restore starter chips on first load, and re-run the same viewport checks.

blockers.description: Sending a normal user message triggers a visible server 500 error from /api/agent-chat; the UI transcript shows an error and blocks end-to-end chat.
blockers.evidence: POST https://ryujin-os.vercel.app/api/agent-chat returned 500 for the test message; console logged "Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 500 ()"; user-visible transcript shows: "Error 500: A server error has occurred FUNCTION_INVOCATION_FAILED iad1::5kw89-1778550381402-c3a64a633bba" (iPhone SE 375x667). Screenshot: iphone_se_after_real_send.png.
blockers.fix: Back-end /api/agent-chat should handle failures gracefully and return a customer-friendly message; implement error handling to avoid exposing internal IDs and ensure a valid/empty reply is shown when the service is unavailable.

blockers.description: Starter scenario chips are not visible on first load due to boot logic clearing the transcript before chips render; the empty-state polish did not land in the live UI.
blockers.evidence: summary evidence shows chips: count=0 container=null across iPhone SE 375x667, iPhone 14 Pro 393x852, Android 360x800, and keyboard/landscape states; test data indicate maybeBoot() clears transcript before chips render.
blockers.fix: Preserve or re-insert the #ry-agent-scenarios chips after boot so chips appear on first load; ensure tapping a chip fills the textarea and sending hides chips as intended.
blockers.location: ry-agent-scenarios container within the transcript block

polish.description: Input row buttons stretch to match the textarea height (44px on mobile, 48px on tablet).
polish.fix: Adjusted .ry-agent-input-row alignment to stretch and set min-height 44px for inputs and 44–48px for action buttons; verified measurements show 44px heights on mobile and 48px on landscape/tablet.

polish.description: Avatar and name compacted into a horizontal identity header (avatar reduced to 56px).
polish.fix: CSS adjustments to .ry-agent-avatar and .ry-agent-identity to render a single compact header (no hero-dominant avatar).

polish.description: Chip clipping addressed by wrapping chips instead of overflow:auto.
polish.fix: CSS landed: .ry-agent-scenarios { display:flex; flex-wrap:wrap; gap:6px; } and .ry-agent-scenario remains compact; ensure chips reflow gracefully.

polish.description: Chips rendering behavior adjusted to be cohesive on first load and not float as floating chrome.
polish.fix: Retain chip container rendering after boot and ensure scenario chips reappear on first load; wire tap on chips to populate input.

polish.description: Header utility unification with gradient backdrop for a cohesive top bar.
polish.fix: Maintain the unified header bar with gradient and safe-area padding; consider subtle border to reinforce cohesion.

strengths: The input row balance is consistently preserved across tested viewports (mobile 44px; landscape/tablet 48px);
Avatar header is compact and non-dominant, improving focus on chat content;
Chip wrapping CSS exists and will prevent overflow in long prompts once chips render on first load;
Header gradient backdrop provides a cohesive top chrome that visually unifies the agent shell.
